content: AI-OS is a deterministic OS-inspired runtime for AI-assisted software engineering. Core thesis: LLMs think, the Kernel decides. 5 layers: Intent, Governance, Kernel (deterministic), Execution (stateless workers), Persistence. Request lifecycle: Goal → Plan → Manifest → Worker → Diff → Reviewer + Guardian → Apply → Done. Kernel is Rust, never calls an LLM. Workers are stateless Python. All writes mediated by Kernel via diff submission.

Polyglot architecture: Rust Kernel (tokio, axum, serde, sqlx, git2, clap), Python for LLM-heavy work (planner, workers, intelligence), Next.js+TS for dashboard. SQLite→PostgreSQL, FAISS→pgvector. Tree-sitter for parsing (never AI). Event bus: tokio channels → Redis Streams → NATS. Traits/interfaces for all cross-subsystem communication. No gRPC until Stage 3.

Stage 1 build order: Phase 1 (Foundation): project scaffold, JSON schemas, State Machine Engine (pure Rust, 14 states, forward+failure transitions), in-process Event Bus (tokio broadcast). Phase 2 (Core Loop): Objective model + SQLite storage, Execution Manifest construction (PIL stubbed), stateless CLI worker, basic Reviewer. Phase 3 (Apply & Recover): Diff Applier (git2), Rollback Manager (git2 snapshot/restore). Phase 4 (Integration): wire end-to-end loop, basic metrics.
